name: David Vano's Caesar Salad
recipeIngredient: 5 tbsp olive oil (less expensive, more pressed oil preferred)
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut a 1-inch strip off the bottom of a medium head of romaine lettuce.
2. Cut the entire head into 1-inch slices and place in cold water to let sit while preparing the dressing.
3. In a large wooden bowl, combine:
- 3 tbsp anchovy paste
- 1 whole egg (coddled)
- Juice of 1/2 lemon
- 4 tbsp balsamic vinegar
- 5 tbsp olive oil
- 1-2 cloves garlic, pressed
- 1/2 tsp seasoned salt
- 1 tbsp Lea and Perrin
- 2-3 drops Tobasco sauce
- 2-3 tbsp fresh grated Parmesan cheese
4. Mix the garlic and anchovy paste with all liquids in the bowl.
5. Add the remaining ingredients, including croutons, to the dressing. Let sit for a few seconds so the croutons absorb some of the liquid.
6. Thoroughly dry the lettuce using a salad spinner if available, then add it to the bowl.
7. Toss until all lettuce is coated in the dressing.
8. Sprinkle with freshly ground pepper and additional croutons before serving.
Serve immediately. Do not make ahead; the salad will not be good after 1-2 hours. If any lettuce remains, discard as romaine lettuce can become bitter if left over.
